name: iaxflood
title: iaxflood

commands.name: iaxflood
commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# iaxflood 192.168.1.202 192.168.1.1 500
Will flood port 4569 from port 4569 500 times
We have IP_HDRINCL

examples.command:
iaxflood 192.168.1.202 192.168.1.1 500
Will flood port 4569 from port 4569 500 times
We have IP_HDRINCL

binary_metadata.file_type: ELF 64-bit LSB pie executable, x86-64, version 1 (SYSV), dynamically linked, interpreter /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2, BuildID[sha1]=b6db9506cd4f04f80e28e5ee76b565b864e5025d, for GNU/Linux 3.2.0, stripped

apt_metadata.description:
VoIP flooder tool
A UDP Inter-Asterisk_eXchange (i.e. IAX) packet was captured
from an IAX channel between two Asterisk IP PBX's. The
content of that packet is the source of the payload for the
attack embodied by this tool. While the IAX protocol header
might not match the Asterisk PBX you'll attack with this
tool, it may require more processing on the part of the PBX
than a simple udpflood without any payload that even
resembles an IAX payload.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use iaxflood because ### iaxflood.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `iaxflood sourcename destinationname numpackets` as documented upstream for iaxflood.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair iaxflood with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `iaxflood --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### iaxflood.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install iaxflood
Install with: sudo apt install iaxflood
